text.tspan: A: To permute the characters in s to match the
order in order, we need to fill a map and two
1. orderMap to map characters to their index in
order. We populate this in a first pass.
2. present to store the characters in s that are
also in s (here orderMap comes handy).
3. remaining to store the characters in s that
Then, sort the chars in present using orderMap[c]
present = [c for c in s if c in orderMap]
remaining = [c for c in s if c not in orderMap]
18. 921. Minimum Add to Make Parentheses Valid
A: Set a couple of variables openP and closingP
to keep track of open and closing parentheses.
Traverse s from left to right adding 1 to openP
every time an open parentheses is found. If a
closing parenthesis is found and openP is greater
than 0, substract 1 from openP. Otherwise, add 1
In the end openP will contain the number of un-
matched open parentheses and closingP the number
Thus, the total number of unmatched parentheses
is given by openP + closingP. This is also the
minimum number of parentheses that need to be
added to s to make it valid.
and 2 close. Thus, the minimum number of paren-
theses to make s valid is 4.
A: Use BFS to traverse all the different remainders of s after substracting the characters of each
sticker, if there are still characters in s after each substraction, repeat the process with all
the stickers once again keeping track of how many substractions have been performed at any given
time (how deep the BFS tree is). If s becomes empty after substracting a sticker, return how many
substractions have been performed up until this point.
Note: To prevent infinite recursion, you need to determine if {c | c in s} ⊆ {c | c in stickers}.
Result: s becomes empty after 3 sticker substractions: ["with", "with", "example"]
